title: Add JSX triage tabs to the React todo fixture
summary: Introduced triage tabs in the React todo JSX fixture to split review work into inbox, active, and done states without leaving one screen.
goal: Seed the frontend handoff corpus with realistic JSX feature work that shares files and rationale across multiple sessions.
changesMade: Added stateful tabs for inbox, active, and done groupings
Restructured App.jsx rendering to support filtered task lists
Kept interactions lightweight so later sessions can layer more controls
Documented the tab behavior in the demo narrative

features.name: triage tabs in JSX
features.why: This fixture needs believable frontend sessions that touch the same React surface for later handoff and conflict inspection.

features.name: list filtering state
features.why: Later sessions will build on the same JSX state model for search, edit, and bulk actions.

features.name: fixture story alignment
features.why: The demo markdown should explain how the frontend sessions relate when inspected from the handoff repo.

decisions.decision: Use the todo fixture as the JSX side of the demo corpus
decisions.why: The repo already contains a React JSX app that is small enough to reason about and rich enough to create overlapping sessions.

decisions.decision: Keep tabs simple so later sessions can layer actions without rewriting the foundation
decisions.why: The point is to create believable session history, not one overdesigned fixture step.

openQuestions: Should the tab state live in the URL once the fixture starts simulating deeper review flows?
testsRun: npm run test -- react todo fixture mental model
resumePrompt: Continue the JSX triage flow in fixtures/react-todo-conflict/src/App.jsx and then sync the updated behavior back into REACT_TODO_CONFLICT_DEMO.md.
